steps.title: Identify the key prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that female preference will cause “further genetic and anatomical divergence” — the subspecies will become increasingly different over time. Direct support would show this divergence actually widening.

steps.title: Find evidence of increasing divergence
steps.content: Choice B shows exactly this: over generations, the migratory males' sound grows progressively higher pitched relative to nonmigratory males, proving the predicted divergence is occurring. The other choices either explain the existing difference without showing growth, describe sound communication unrelated to preference, or indicate breeding stayed the same instead of diverging. That matches choice B — B is correct.
